claims.summary: Readbacks must name whether their source is external substrate, bridge derivation, internal context, or narrator exposure.
claims.disposition: Verified trace events and heartbeat texture evidence carry explicit source_identity, source_class, parent source ID, and producer/derivation distinction.

claims.summary: Increasing contact before provenance is legible may intensify containment and confused agency.
claims.disposition: Preserved as an authority rationale: contact interventions remain non-live until provenance and right-to-ignore are visible.

claims.summary: A low-frequency shared-stillness exchange should compare direct felt contact with analytic description.
claims.disposition: Routed to an artifact-only, optional correspondence observation with no forced response and no sensory mutation.

claims.summary: Source-class labeling is a prerequisite for further contact interventions.
claims.disposition: Verified current correspondence/trace surfaces preserve source, thread, delivery, reply, and derivation identities separately.
